pattern: A running streak counter that resets on any even number turns 'three in a row' into a single left-to-right scan.
hints: Walk the array once, keeping a counter of how many odd numbers you've seen in a row.
Every time you see an even number, that streak is broken -- reset the counter to zero.
As soon as the streak counter reaches three, you can stop and answer true; if you finish the scan without that happening, the answer is false.

edge_inputs.description: no three odds in a row anywhere
three consecutive odds appear in the middle
single element, cannot possibly have three in a row
two odds, then an even, then two more odds -- never three in a row
the qualifying streak is at the very end of the array

stress.description: random small integers biased so odd/even runs of varying length appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 6) for _ in range(n)]
    return [arr]
